app.browserOnlyBackend: You’re running the frontend only (npm run dev). Launch Rotate with npm run desktop to use the vault and integrations.

sidebar.footerHint: Everything stays on this device. Lock the session when you are done.

propagation.title: Propagate secret
propagation.description: Choose the destinations to align with the new value.
propagation.batchLead: You can update Vercel, a local env file, and GitHub Actions in the same step.
propagation.vercelDescription: Writes {{value}} to the project's env vars.
propagation.connectVercel: Connect Vercel from Explore to write this value to project env vars.
propagation.localEnvLead: Point to an .env file: Rotate reads only variable names and writes the new value.
propagation.githubLead: Writes the value to Secrets in the selected GitHub repository.
propagation.connectGithub: Connect GitHub from Explore to write this value to repositories.
propagation.localEnvKeysFound: {{count}} keys found in the env file.
propagation.localEnvWillCreate: The file does not exist yet: Rotate will create it when writing.
propagation.localEnvCreated: Variable {{key}} created in the local env file.
propagation.localEnvUpdated: Variable {{key}} updated in the local env file.

github.description: Connect a GitHub token to write Actions Secrets to repositories after a rotation.
github.connectLead: Use a dedicated token with access to the repositories where you want to update Actions Secrets.

guide.step1: Create a fine-grained personal access token from GitHub Developer settings.
guide.step3: Enable at least Repository permissions: Secrets read/write and Metadata read.
guide.step4: Paste the token here: Rotate stores it in the local keyring.

github.linkedLead: GitHub will be used as a destination for Actions Secrets during rotations.

manualProviders.secretShownAfterSave: The secret is shown here only after saving, so you can propagate it immediately.
manualProviders.secretOptional: You can leave this empty on the first connection and add it when you generate the new key.
manualProviders.resultLead: Store or propagate this value immediately. The provider may not show it again.
manualProviders.copied: Secret copied to the temporary clipboard.
manualProviders.secretSaved: Secret saved. Now update the linked destinations.
manualProviders.referenceSaved: Reference saved. Add the secret when you generate it from the provider.

stripe.title: Stripe account
stripe.description: Manage assisted replacement of Stripe keys and propagate them to linked environments.
steps: Open Stripe Dashboard -> Developers -> API keys.
Create or rotate a Secret key or Restricted key from the Dashboard. Stripe shows the value once.
Paste the new value here and propagate it before deleting or disabling the previous key.
stripe.publicIdHint: Used as a local reference. The secret key stays in the vault.
stripe.secretLabel: New secret key generated by Stripe

paypal.title: PayPal app
paypal.description: Manage PayPal client secrets generated in the Developer Dashboard and propagate them to linked environments.
steps: Open PayPal Developer Dashboard -> Apps & Credentials.
Select the REST app and generate or copy the new client secret for Sandbox or Live.
Paste the new value here and propagate it before retiring the previous secret.
paypal.publicIdHint: The Client ID is not secret; the client secret stays in the vault.
paypal.secretLabel: New client secret generated by PayPal

facebook.title: Facebook app
facebook.description: Manage Facebook App Secrets generated in Meta Developers Dashboard and propagate them to linked environments.
steps: Open Meta for Developers -> My Apps and select the app.
Go to App settings -> Basic and use Show or Reset next to App Secret.
Paste the new App Secret here and propagate it before updating services that use the old one.
facebook.publicIdHint: The App ID is not secret; the App Secret stays in the vault.
facebook.secretLabel: New App Secret generated by Facebook

discord.title: Discord app
discord.description: Manage Discord bot tokens and client secrets generated in the Developer Portal and propagate them to linked environments.
steps: Open Discord Developer Portal -> Applications and select the app.
For a bot token, go to Bot -> Reset Token. For OAuth, go to OAuth2 and regenerate/copy the client secret.
Paste the new value here and propagate it before retiring the previous one.
discord.publicIdHint: The Application ID is not secret; bot tokens and client secrets stay in the vault.
discord.secretLabel: New Discord bot token or client secret

twitch.title: Twitch app
twitch.description: Manage Twitch client secrets generated in the Developer Console and propagate them to linked environments.
steps: Open Twitch Developer Console -> Applications and select your app.
Create or regenerate the client secret from the app's Manage area.
Paste the new client secret here and propagate it before updating services that use the old one.
twitch.publicIdHint: The Client ID is not secret; the client secret stays in the vault.
twitch.secretLabel: New client secret generated by Twitch

googleOauth.description: Store the client secret generated by Google and propagate it to deploy env vars.

guide.step1: Open Google Auth Platform -> Clients and select the OAuth client.
guide.step2: Use Add Secret to generate a new client secret. Google shows it only once.
guide.step3: Paste the new secret here and update Vercel or other environments before disabling the old secret.

googleOauth.saveGeneratedSecret: Save the new secret generated by Google
googleOauth.connectLead: The Google Client ID is enough to connect the client. Add the secret later, when you generate it with Add Secret in Google Console.
googleOauth.secretHint: You can leave this empty on the first connection. When rotating, paste the secret just created with Add Secret in Google Console.
googleOauth.resultLead: This is the value generated by Google and saved in Rotate. Update linked env vars immediately.
googleOauth.secretSaved: Google secret saved. Now update the linked env vars.
googleOauth.clientLinked: OAuth client linked. Add the secret when you generate it from Google.

resend.description: Manage Resend API keys and write the new value to your deploy env vars.

guide.step2: Create or copy an API key with enough permission to read, create, and delete API keys.
guide.step3: Paste it here: Rotate will use it only as a local management credential.

resend.keysLead: Resend shows the new token only once. Propagate it before deleting the previous key.
resend.resultLead: Copy or write this value now: Resend will not show it again.

cloudflareTurnstile.resultLead: Update the backend that uses this widget immediately. Cloudflare shows this secret only now.
cloudflareTurnstile.propagationLead: Select Vercel, Supabase, local env, and GitHub to align destinations in the same step.
cloudflareTurnstile.localEnvLead: Point to an .env file: Rotate reads only variable names and writes the new secret.
cloudflareTurnstile.githubLead: Writes the new value to Secrets in the selected GitHub repository.
cloudflareTurnstile.workerLead: The value is written to Cloudflare Workers and is not saved in the local database.
cloudflareTurnstile.pagesLead: Pages uses this value in later deploys/builds.
cloudflareTurnstile.secretsStoreLead: If you do not select an existing secret, Rotate creates a new one in the selected store.

vercel.description: Connect a Vercel Access Token to update project environment variables.
vercel.connectLead: Create a token from Vercel Account Settings. If you work in a team, also enter the Team ID.

guide.step1: Open Vercel Account Settings and create an Access Token dedicated to Rotate.
guide.step2: Select the correct scope for the personal or team projects you want to update.
guide.step3: If the project belongs to a team, also copy the Team ID from Team Settings -> General.
guide.step4: Paste the token here: Rotate uses it to list projects and update environment variables.

supabase.description: Connect a Supabase Personal Access Token to rotate API keys and update Edge Function secrets.
supabase.connectLead: Create a Personal Access Token with access to projects and Edge Function secrets.

guide.step1: Open Supabase Account -> Access Tokens and create a Personal Access Token dedicated to Rotate.
guide.step2: The token must be able to read projects, read/write API keys, and read/write Edge Function secrets.
guide.step3: API keys are generated by Supabase; Rotate shows the new value only once.
guide.step4: Edge Function secrets do not generate values: they receive values produced by another rotation or pasted explicitly.

supabase.loadingDescription: Downloading available projects, API keys, and secrets.
supabase.databaseUrlCopied: DATABASE_URL copied to the temporary clipboard.
supabase.passwordCopied: Password copied to the temporary clipboard.
supabase.customSecretsLead: This section shows only application secrets already present in Supabase and not managed by the dedicated flows above. You can update them together or create a new one by pasting a value generated elsewhere, for example a Turnstile secret.
supabase.noCustomSecretsLead: You can create one from the panel on the right by entering name and value.
supabase.updateSecretLead: Save the same value to selected secrets, or also create a new manual secret.
supabase.manualSecretHint: Optional: use it only if the secret does not appear in the table yet.
supabase.externalValueHint: Paste a value already generated by another service, for example a new Turnstile secret.
supabase.apiKeysLead: Here the new key is generated by Supabase. Rotate shows it only once.
supabase.databasePasswordLead: Rotate the password of the main Postgres user and generate a new direct DATABASE_URL. Supabase updates managed services, but your external services must be updated immediately.
supabase.selectProjectForDb: Select a project before rotating the database password.
supabase.apiKeyResultLead: Supabase shows this value only now. Update services that used the previous key.
supabase.databaseUrlResultLead: Immediately update pools, backends, and deploys that use the old password. Rotate does not save these values.

settings.title: Vault preferences
settings.subtitle: Tune how the app behaves on this device.
settings.sessionLead: When the timer expires, Rotate asks for your password before opening the vault again.
settings.sessionHint: The maximum is 8 hours. Saving realigns the current session to the new duration.
settings.passwordLead: Your password protects the local vault. Rotate cannot show it or recover it for you.
settings.passwordHint: Use at least {{min}} characters. Below 16 characters, use at least 3 categories among lowercase, uppercase, numbers, and symbols.
settings.passwordWeak: The new password must have at least {{min}} characters and be less predictable.
settings.passwordMismatch: The two new passwords do not match.
settings.passwordSame: The new password must be different from the current one.

home.emptyDescription: Pick a provider in Explore and connect it to your workspace. You rotate only the assets you choose—nothing else in the account is touched.
home.withServicesSubtitle: Open any account below for keys, rotation, and connection settings.

explore.title: Which providers to manage here
explore.subtitle: Add only what you actually use. Every rotation is scoped: one resource at a time, never a blind reset of the whole account.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare
cloudflare.blurb: Link Account ID and management token: list tokens, rotate with the same policies, secrets stored securely.

vercel.title: Vercel
vercel.blurb: Connect a Vercel token to update project environment variables after a rotation.

supabase.title: Supabase
supabase.blurb: Project keys and tenant secrets. When supported, sensitive actions stay behind the vault.

resend.title: Resend
resend.blurb: Create new Resend API keys, reveal the value once, and update linked env vars.

oauth_google.title: OAuth (Google)
oauth_google.blurb: Assisted handling for Client ID and client secrets created in Google Cloud Console.

github.title: GitHub
github.blurb: Write Actions Secrets to repositories after a rotation.

stripe.title: Stripe
stripe.blurb: Assisted handling for Stripe secret keys generated in the Dashboard and propagation to env vars.

paypal.title: PayPal
paypal.blurb: Assisted handling for PayPal client secrets generated in the Developer Dashboard.

facebook.title: Facebook
facebook.blurb: Assisted handling for Facebook App Secrets generated in Meta for Developers.

discord.title: Discord
discord.blurb: Assisted handling for Discord bot tokens and client secrets generated in the Developer Portal.

twitch.title: Twitch
twitch.blurb: Assisted handling for Twitch client secrets generated in the Developer Console.

services.empty: No services yet. Go to Explore to connect your first account.
services.title: Your accounts
services.subtitle: Click a row to open the account and manage tokens and settings.
services.removeBody: Remove {{provider}} "{{label}}" from Rotate? Local credentials, metadata, and saved settings for this service will be deleted. External provider resources are not deleted.

placeholder.body: Rotate doesn’t support this provider yet. Full controls—account linking, keys, and rotation—will ship in a later update.

vault.setupBody: This password unlocks the local vault. If you lose it, Rotate cannot open saved integrations or secrets.
vault.pinMin: The password must be at least {{min}} characters.
vault.pinMismatch: The two passwords do not match.

changePin.title: Change vault password
changePin.newMin: The new password must be at least {{min}} characters and less predictable.
changePin.mismatch: The two new password entries do not match.

cloudflare.intro: The management token is stored locally by Rotate. To list and rotate tokens you need at least <0>Account · API Tokens · Read</0> and <1>Edit</1> (create and delete).
cloudflare.connectLead: This key is used only by Rotate to read and create Cloudflare tokens.
cloudflare.reconnectLead: The account is already known, but the internal token required to list and rotate keys is missing.
cloudflare.keyringHint: Rotate saves this credential on this device and prefers the system keyring when available.
cloudflare.rotateByIdLead: If the token does not appear in the list, paste its Cloudflare ID here. Rotate will clone its policies, show the new secret, and can revoke the old token.
cloudflare.noTokens: No tokens returned. Check that the management token has the right permissions.
cloudflare.footnote: Rotation creates a new token with the same policies. The secret is shown only once—copy it now. Rotate's management token stays separate from tracked tokens.
cloudflare.confirmUnlink: Remove the Account ID and token from this device
cloudflare.confirmReveal: Anyone who can see your screen can read the token. Continue
cloudflare.rotateLead: A new token will be created with the same policies as <0>{{name}}</0>.
cloudflare.rotateUpdateVault: Update the token saved in Rotate (required if you rotate the token this app uses for Cloudflare).
cloudflare.rotateRevoke: Revoke the previous token after the new one is created.
cloudflare.rotateWarn: Before revoking the old token, update the destinations where it is used. Management-token rotation will use a separate flow.
cloudflare.resultLead: Store this value now—the Cloudflare dashboard will not show it again.
cloudflare.revealLead: Use in a private setting—don’t share your screen. Close as soon as you’re done.
cloudflare.clipboardHint: Clipboard will clear automatically in about 30 seconds.
